steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This Cross-Text Connections question asks for a point both authors would agree on. The shared claim must be supported by both Text 1 and Text 2. Text 1 states that organ pipe cacti provide shade for tepary beans, protecting them from "the harshest heat and light." Text 2 states that O'odham farmers plant tepary beans in the shade of organ pipe cacti "for protection from the sun and heat."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Organ pipe cacti can provide shade that protects tepary beans from high-intensity heat and light.’: Text 1 explicitly says shade "helps protect tepary beans from the harshest heat and light"; Text 2 says organ pipe cacti provide "protection from the sun and heat." Both authors support this. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Compared with Ramos's approach, the O'odham approach has the advantage of producing beans.’: Text 1 never compares the two approaches, and Text 2 does not claim the O'odham approach produces more beans than Ramos's panels do. Neither author makes this comparison. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Organ pipe cacti grow best when planted in shaded areas, while tepary beans do not require shade to thrive.’: This is the opposite of what both texts say — tepary beans grow best when shaded, and organ pipe cacti are described as "sun-hardy." ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Ramos's team's method could be refined to more actively prevent soil moisture from evaporating.’: Only Text 1 mentions soil moisture; Text 2 does not. Neither author makes a claim about refining Ramos's method. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
